36643.name: Orca Habitat
36643.description: The ocean floor descends into a tranquil expanse that serves as a sanctuary for orcas. This habitat, nestled in a calm basin shielded from the strong surface currents, provides a serene contrast to the vibrant activity above. The ocean bed is dotted with large, smooth boulders and sporadic patches of soft, fine sand that create a natural playground for the orcas.

36411.name: Inside the Lighthouse
36411.description: The stone walls match much of the outside's wear and tear. The air is cold and crisp from a slight breeze seemingly coming down from above. A spiral staircase offers a way to ascend the lighthouse, and a wooden door leads out of the building.

36420.name: Beneath the Pier
36420.description: Waves crash against several large wooden pillars that extend down below the surface of the water supporting a platform above. A wooden ladder is built into the side from a platform where smaller vessels may be docked. Seagulls squawk overhead and strut along the sides of the pier above you expectantly.

36468.name: The Preparation Chamber
36468.description: The vestibule is lined with racks with various arms and armor. None very impressive, but they're better than nothing. A couple stone benches circle the center of the room around a central iron brazier lit with a fire. A large lattice gate to the west leads into the arena. A ramp to the south leads up and out of the chamber.

36642.name: The Coral Reef
36642.description: The underwater world unfolds in a vibrant tapestry of life. Here, the water deepens into a rich turquoise, clear and luminous, allowing a spectacular view of the marine landscape below. Vast patches of coral in hues of orange, pink, and purple sprawl across the ocean floor, forming an intricate reef system that teems with activity.

36355.name: Suite 105
36355.description: This room is very large. It stretches from north to south with windows that give the tenant a view of the streets and the beach respectively. A large bed sits against the western wall with dressers and clothes cabinets along the eastern wall. The possessions in this room suggest that it may belong to a couple of nobles. The room exits to the east.

36356.name: Suite 104
36356.description: This room has a gorgeous view of the beach! The bed has been strategically placed against the north wall and aligned with the window to offer a great view of the beach from the comfort of the suite. Dressers sit against the south wall on either side of the window. The belongings in this room suggest that it is rented by one person; a very short person. The room exits to the north.

36357.name: Suite 106
36357.description: The room is a pretty decent size with a window that looks out to the streets of Kulani. A large bed sits against the eastern wall in front of a stunning mahogany headboard. A couple dressers sit against the wall on either side of the entrance to the room. The room has no personal belongings, suggesting that it is a vacant room. The room exits to the south.

36358.name: Suite 102
36358.description: This room is very large. It stretches from north to south with windows that give the tenant a view of the streets and the beach respectively. A large bed sits against the eastern wall with dressers and clothes cabinets along the western wall. There are some fashionable decorations that suggest this room is occupied by a noble. The room exits to the west.

36359.name: Suite 103
36359.description: This room has a gorgeous view of the beach! The bed has been strategically placed against the north wall and aligned with the window to offer a great view of the beach from the comfort of the suite. Dressers sit against the south wall on either side of the window. The belongings in this room suggest it is rented by a family, as childrens clothes are scattered about haphazardly. The room exits to the north.

36360.name: Suite 101
36360.description: The room is a pretty decent size with a window that looks out to the streets of Kulani. A large bed sits against the western wall in front of a stunning mahogany headboard. A couple dressers sit against the wall on either side of the entrance to the room. The room seems occupied with ordinary luggage that a common tourist might bring for their vacation. The room exits to the south.

36364.name: Kulani Suites - 1st Floor
36364.description: The carpeted eastern hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the west, where the inn's lobby leads out of the resort.

36365.name: Kulani Suites - 1st Floor
36365.description: The carpeted western hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the east, where the inn's lobby leads out of the resort.

36366.name: Kulani Suites - 1st Floor
36366.description: Extravagant! A red carpet lines the floor trailing east to west where the inn offers rooms to its patrons. The staircase before you, made entirely out of immaculately cut marble, leads upwards to the second floor. The hallway continues east and west here, leading to the suites on the first floor.

36367.name: Kulani Inn
36367.description: The Kulani Inn lives up to its reputation as one of the most desirable resort destinations as you are greeted with carpeted flooring above a marble foundation. A large mahogany reception desk sits in the center of the room offering patrons of the island refuge for their stay. The lobby continues south where a visible intricate staircase can be seen, while the north leads out of the inn.

36368.name: Path to the Northern Shore
36368.description: The clanging of steel immediately fills your ears as a battle ensues before you; it's almost deafening. The once glittering sands of Kulani is now being spattered with blood at your feet leading down to the beach. Mahogany and koa trees line the path on either side of you. Glancing into the trees, you can barely make out humanoid figures with bows drawn between the swaying outer branches. A mixutre of men passing you by head north into battle, while wounded men retreat south into the city for aid. The leadership here seems very unorganized.

36369.name: Before the Archway
36369.description: The massive stone arch is accompanied by walls that stretch east and west around the perimeter of the city. Men, elves and dwarves are intermixed among the crowd of soldiers. Finding a way under the arch might prove difficult as a continuous flow of soldiers flow in and out of the city. Men can be spotted standing guard at the base of the arch, while elves find perch on top of the walls with bows drawn. North leads out of the city into more commotion, while south offers a more appealing escape from the bustle of war.

36370.name: Northern Streets of Kulani
36370.description: The noise level grows louder here as a mixture of men shouting orders is combined with the cries of the wounded. You begin to notice spots of blood at your feet leading east into a makeshift hospital. The clanging of steel from the smithery to the west adds to the commotion causing your sense of hearing to overload from the constant barrage of noise. The cobblestone path stretches north to a massive arch, and south to the town center.

36371.name: Northern Streets of Kulani
36371.description: The change in tone is immediately noticable. There are less tourists, and more armed mercenaries occupying the cobblestone path here. Your presence goes noticed as well-equipped men size you up as they pass by. South leads to the town center, while north is cluttered with more soldiers preparing for, or retreating from a battle. To the east and west are stone buildings that offer basic good for the everyday traveler.

36372.name: Western Streets of Kulani
36372.description: The stone architecture is prominent here more than ever. A large building stands to your south with multiple windows, signifying some sort of inn. An enormous arch lines the path to a massive circular stone structure to the west. The path to the north decends to a plateau, while east leads to the town center.

36373.name: Western Streets of Kulani
36373.description: The cobblestone path is lined with mahogany flower boxes against the northern and southern buildings displaying a beautiful assortment of plumeria and small hibiscus flower bushes. Tall marble poles also line the sides of the street for other seasonal decorative purposes. The cobblestone path leads east and west, while two businesses can be found to the north and south.

36374.name: Kulani Bazaar District
36374.description: Bazaar tents lead all the way up to the eastern walls of Kulani here, even attaching to them. Between the incessant banter of haggling, children whining and the performance of street musicians, it would be claustrophobic to some people. The east leads under an arch exiting the city, while west leads to more shopping.

36375.name: Kulani Bazaar District
36375.description: The sudden increase in the noise level of toursits and haggling signifies that you are in the bazaar streets of Kulani. Tents are erected on either side of the cobblestone path, while street performers perform various acts to entertain the tourists during their shopping experience. Seemingly dozens of conversations are happening at once. Tents of all shapes and sizes are strewn about, leading further east. To the west stands a tall fountain displayed at the town center.

36376.name: Kulani Town Center
36376.description: A crossroad of streets extend in all directions here. In the center sits a massive ornate fountain which protrudes water from its center showering down to its lower tiers. The sound of flowing water muffles the banter of passersby. South offers a route to the shore accompanied by decorated flagpoles adorned with gold lacings that spiral upwards. Immediately to the east lies the shopping bazaar designed to attract tourists. West offers more accommodations, while the path leading north is bustling with heavily armed guards trampling the cobblestone path.

36377.name: Southern Streets of Kulani
36377.description: The cobblestone path is adorned with tall columned mahogany flower pots with drooping ferns. Tents of various shapes, sizes and colors line the path while the hoots and hollars of sales pitches can be heard in all directions. Why not pick up a souvenir? The cobblestone path continues south to a massive arch, and north leads to the town center. A stone building decorated with window boxes stands to the west. To the east, a lattice fence covered in ivy runs parallel to the street.

36378.name: Southern Streets of Kulani
36378.description: The path is accompanied by towering flagpoles with waving flags of all walks of life. Flags representing Mareldja, Crown on the Water, is accompanied alongside the Ramluf Cadain dwarves, the Decaren Jorel explorers, the Barcus entrepreneurs and even a beatifully embroidered flag of Isvaelin waves proudly among them. An overwhelming sense of diversified unification stands as a testament to the future between these long-forgotten allegiances. The streets lead north to the town center, and south to a massive arch. A smaller stone path leads east, and a building with enticing beach gear is to the west.

36379.name: On the Ocean
36379.description: Surfs up! The strong current of the ocean fights to push you back east against the shore. The crystalline sea-green water is clear enough that you can see various tropical fish swimming around patches of coral beneath you. The ocean stretches as far as the eye can see, with the shores of Kulani to your east.

36380.name: Kulani's Royal Archway
36380.description: Before you stands a large stone archway with chiseled divots that spiral upwards. Smooth stone walls extend to the east and west encasing the city. The stone path widens passed the arch to the north, while the cobblestone trail narrows to the south, ending at the beach.

36381.name: Path to Kulani
36381.description: The sandy path leading from the beach narrows into a trail covered by the shade of koa and mahogany trees. Seagulls and other various birds can be seen flapping from limb to limb overhead. The trail continues north, while south leads to the beaches of Kulani.

36382.name: Before the Archway
36382.description: A large stone archway offers entrance to the streets of Kulani to the west. To the south, a narrow dirt path can be found; perhaps an old fisherman's trail. Stone walls extend to the north and south which are immediately concealed in thick layers of ferns and underbrush. The cobblestone path extends east and west here.

36383.name: Western Shore
36383.description: The waves are more prominent here, as the water swells in from the west. You can spot a couple surfers out in the ocean waiting for that perfect wave. Sea- green water continually crashes against the shoreline, putting your mind at ease with a susurrous melody. West leads to the ocean swells and the beach stretches out to the east.

36384.name: On the Beach
36384.description: The only thing you hear is waves crashing. Not many tourists come this way to set up for a day at the beach. The glistening sands at your feet have been smoothed out by the water and lack of foot traffic in this direction. The beach continues to the west and east.

36385.name: On the Beach
36385.description: What a view! Gentle swells of sea-green water crashes in from the south and creeping up to your feet. This would be a perfect spot to set up a beach chair and enjoy the scenery. The beach continues to the west with the pier to the east.

36386.name: Shadowy Trail
36386.description: The trail is less dense in shrubbery here, giving you a clear view of the path to the north which opens up to stone road with a large arched enterance. The trail must have been intentionally undisturbed to keep the wildlife unaffected. How thoughtful. The trail narrows to the south and opens up to the north.

36387.name: Rocky Shoreline
36387.description: Waves crash loudly against the protruding rocks along the shore. The beach ends, and you are instead met with large rock formations that stop any further travel to the north. The surface of the rocks are littered with oysters, while other molluscs and various sea anemones inhabit the surrounding waters.

36388.name: Eastern Shore
36388.description: The shore here is littered with rocks and splintered driftwood. Less populated with tourists, the native wildlife find refuge on this side of the island. The beach clutter is cleaner to the south while the rocky shore extends north. To the west lies a trail leading under the cover of koa and mahogany trees.

36389.name: Eastern Shore
36389.description: The shore bends to the north here as gentle waves creep in the from the east. The harmonic rhythm of the ocean waves are almost hypnotizing, beckoning you to take a few moments to relax and enjoy the scenery. The beach extends far to the west and continues north here.

36390.name: Sandy Beach Path
36390.description: The trail is a narrow path with scattered ferns strewn about making travel a bit strenuous. Shade from the tall koa and mahogany trees block any sunlight that would otherwise lighten the path. The trail continues to the west, while the east leads to the beaches of Kulani.

36391.name: Shadowy Trail
36391.description: The sandy path that trails away from the beach quickly turns in to a road of dirt and foliage quite abruptly. The path narrows as dense ferns hinder your progress. Maybe you should have brought a machete. The path widens to the beach to the south, while to the east and north the trail offers much of the same.

36393.name: On the Beach
36393.description: The beach population grows sparse this far out, but a small hut stands before you made entirely out of koa wood. Heavy foot traffic disturbs the otherwise pristine layer of sand throughout the rest of the beach. The beach continues east and west here.

36394.name: On the Beach
36394.description: The sands glisten all around you, what a sight! The ocean swells in from the south as you witness children splashing around in the ocean water. Blankets and beach chairs are strewn about as far as the eye can see. A trail away from the beach leads north, and the beach continues to the east and west here.

36395.name: On the Beach
36395.description: Walking along the beach you notice a few beachgoers set up with blankets and umbrellas, enjoying the weather. Children are playing in the water and making various sand castles. The water swells gently up to your feet, tempting you for a quick dip in the ocean water. What's stopping you? The beach continues east and west here.

36396.name: On the Beach
36396.description: With the pier off to your west, the ocean waves gently swell in from the south. Tiny sea crabs scuttle about and dig into the sand as the waves knock them around. The sun cascades over the glistening sands, offering a warm pleasant feeling of perfect beach weather. Why not kick off your shoes and enjoy the sand? The beach continues to the east and west here.

36397.name: On the Pier
36397.description: The pier extends a ways out to the ocean where boats may be docked. The base of the pier offers steps up to the mahogany platform, sand is strewn about the steps kicked up from the beach. Platinum railings on either side of you offer a steady ascent or descent. The south leads out to the dock while the beach leads in all directions.

36398.name: The Boardwalk
36398.description: Twinkling sands glisten in the sunlight beneath the boardwalk on either side of you. Benches line the sides offering quick refuge behind the rails to travelers as they would either wait for their ferry or gather their bearings before entering the city. The pier extends to the south where ships may be docked. To the north the boardwalk leads to the sandy shores of Kulani.

36399.name: The Ornamented Pier
36399.description: Kulani spared no expense right out of the gate, or pier in this case. The boards beneath your feet are made from mahogany and polished to withstand the punishment of heavy traffic. Upon inspection, the railings on the perimeter of the landing on either side of you are made entirely of platinum poles engraved with precious gemstones. North leads to more mahogany boards lined with gaudy railings leading to the shore.

36400.name: Before the Jail
36400.description: The structure has thick iron bars over the windows, and an extremely sturdy large steel door which looks rather intimidating. Glancing around, you notice the structure is very well out of sight from the rest of the city, as if by design. North leads along the wall of the western archway, and an outside staircase leads down under the building.

36402.name: Suite Facilities
36402.description: Marble flooring covers the ground here with lattice paneling that lines the walls with various ivy plants coating them. The room has a very pleasant aroma of blooming jasmine. Two doors against the south wall lead to locker rooms separated by gender; southwest for men, southeast for women. North leads to the Inn's vestibule.

36403.name: Women's Locker Room
36403.description: Two rows of lockers line the eastern side of the room while the western wall bears the washroom facilities for the Inn's female patrons. The marble flooring is clean and well kept. A fresh, flowery scent subdues any other foul odors that may come from a room such as this. Northwest leads out of the locker room while south leads further in to the bathing facilities.

36404.name: Men's Locker Room
36404.description: Two rows of lockers line the western side of the room while the eastern wall bears the washroom facilities for the Inn's male patrons. The marble flooring is clean and well kept. A fresh, flowery scent subdues any other foul odors that may come from a room such as this. Northeast leads out of the locker room while south leads further in to the bathing facilities.

36405.name: Women's Bath House
36405.description: Several large pools of heated water line the walls separated by wooden lattices covered in jasmine plants growing all around them. The room is quite humid from the heat as air barely escapes through the linear windows high against the south wall. The bathing area exits to the north.

36406.name: Men's Bath House
36406.description: Several large pools of heated water line the walls separated by wooden lattices covered in jasmine plants growing all around them. The room is quite humid from the heat as air barely escapes through the linear windows high against the south wall. The bathing area exits to the north.

36407.name: Path to the Eastern Cliffs
36407.description: The path is surrounded by dense ferns and koa trees to the north and south, blocking vision in either direction. The fresh smell of salt and brine flows in from the east accompanied by the sound of crashing waves. The strong ocean breeze through the brush and trees create a very tranquil psithurism. Kulani's large archway can be seen to the west.

36408.name: Eastern Cliffs
36408.description: The rocky outcropping oversees the eastern shore of the island. If you're not careful, the strong ocean breeze sweeping in from the east could cause you to fall down to the rocky shore below. A lighthouse can be seen to the north, a rocky shoreline below you extends to the south and a narrow dirt path surrounded by ferns leads to the west.

36409.name: Overgrown Path
36409.description: The overgrown path is surrounded by dense ferns and a thick layer of foliage. The air seems calm as the scent of salt and brine sweeps in from the east. A vague indication of broken branches lead to the east.

36410.name: Before the Lighthouse
36410.description: Before you stands a tall lighthouse overlooking the eastern shore protruding on a rocky outlook. Oddly, the tower seems a bit worn and unkept from the outside. A wooden door offers an entrance to the tower while west leads to an overgrown makeshift pathway.

36412.name: Lighthouse - 2nd Floor
36412.description: The stone walls holding up the spiral staircase are so cold you can feel it permeating just by being nearby. The spiral staircase descends both upwards to an opening above to a platform and downwards to the lighthouse's bottom floor. A gentle breeze smelling of salt and brine flows down into the lighthouse from above, and the sound of crashing waves can be heard coming from above as well.

36465.name: Behind the Mahogany Workbench
36465.description: Behind the solid wooden workbench, you notice a pristine set of jewelers tools at one end. The tools include dozens of magnifying glasses of different sizes laid in a box lined with soft velvet. Beautiful gemstones are neatly arranged across the workbench. Candy wrappers litter the far end of the table. South leads to the entrance of the golden laced white tent.

36268.name: Path to the Northern Shore
36268.description: The trees and ferns here appear scratched or otherwise battered down as the heavy foot traffic tramples the sandy pathway. The verbal commotion grows louder to the north, continuing with the chaos. South leads against the flow of traffic and eventually towards Kulani's northern archway.

36269.name: Path to the Northern Shore
36269.description: No time to enjoy the scenery unless you want to risk getting knocked over. Armed men and women push their way to the north as a few retreat south. The wounds on some of these deserters look particularly gruesome. Spats of blood coat the sands, giving off a sparkling red hue. The bustle of traffic continues to the north and south here.

36270.name: Path to the Northern Shore
36270.description: The pathway isn't ideal for this kind of traffic. The tall koa trees are accompanied by an extremely thick layer of ferns and shrubs at their base, forcing people to squeeze by one another, sometimes knocking each other over through all the commotion and urgency. The path leads north to more chaos, and south towards a massive archway.

36272.name: Cave Entrance
36272.description: Tall, green ferns waist high coat the ground in all directions. It is so quiet here that you can hear the droplets of water landing on the ferns from where the cave overhangs the entrance. The padded down foliage around you indicate that this area has not been used by man, but by something larger. Much larger. The dismal entrance of the cave offers you no amount of comfort as to what may lie within. Pushing back through the ferns seems like the sensible choice.

36273.name: Shadowy Trail
36273.description: The brush around you sways slightly with a gentle, yet unpredictable breeze. The path opens up to the north, accompanied by the slight howl of something massively hollow in response to the breeze. A cramped trail of dense ferns are slightly beaten to the southeast, indicating a narrow path of travel, if you

36274.name: Shadowy Trail
36274.description: A tiny bit of light from the sky above manages to shine through, but not enough to help you navigate in any way. The cramped passage between the thick growth of ferns seem to open up slightly to the northwest. To the east, broken branches indicate a narrow path of travel.

36275.name: Shadowy Trail
36275.description: Making your way through the dense ferns is a challenge. Every step is met with the shuffling of smaller critters nearby that scatter from your position. Going unnoticed would prove to be a difficult task for even the stealthiest of rogues. Just pray you don't attract the wrong attention with your clumsy movement along the trail. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

36276.name: Shadowy Trail
36276.description: Not only is it unnaturally dark here, it's quiet. Except for you. The only thing spookier than the overwhelming silence is the occasional beastly cackle and movement of something large in the brush somewhere in the distance. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

36277.name: Shadowy Trail
36277.description: The loose trail of broken branches is accompanied by a deep darkness. The tall brush overshadows you in the middle of a dense forest of koa trees, making it impossible to see the sky. A thick layer of foliage at your feet make it hard to gain any confident footing. The indications of broken branches lead east and west here.

36278.name: Off the Beaten Path
36278.description: Getting through here is a squeeze. Tall, dense ferns surround you in every direction. Indications of bent or otherwise broken branches are signs that this path has been made by something before you. The disturbed brush leads west while east opens up into a sandy path.

36279.name: First Mate's Quarters
36279.description: These quarters open up to racks of weaponry and armor lined against the walls. A table stands against the far wall with a few scribbled notes resting on top. A few weapons are scattered about the room, as if knocked over in a haste. A pile of assorted equipment is strewn around the perimeter of the room haphazardly.

36280.name: Captain's Quarters
36280.description: This room opens up to a large wooden table in the center. A large desk stands against the far corner behind a large chair. A map of an island archipelago rests on top of the desk. Not much in the way of decor, but it is efficient. Oddly, the chair behind the desk rests unevenly on the floor. The room exits to the aft.

36281.name: Below the Port Stern
36281.description: Windows line the walls of the hull and stop at a door towards the bow of the ship. Attached to the door is a large decorative ship wheel mounted on its face. The area extends in a triangular fashion towards the aft and port side here.

36282.name: Below the Starboard Stern
36282.description: Windows line the walls of the hull and stop at a door towards the bow of the ship. Attached to the door is a pair of crossed swords mounted on its face. The area extends in a triangular fashion towards the aft and starboard side here.

36283.name: Below the Stern
36283.description: Small windows line the walls of the hull here, offering a view of the ocean under shelter from the weather. Multiple empty racks line the walls, likely used to holster many weapons and suits of armor. The hull extends port and starboard side here towards the bow of the ship. A staircase leads above deck.

36294.name: Shadowy Trail
36294.description: Trudging through dense shrubs and tall ferns, you find yourself under heavy cover of mahogany and koa trees as their branches blot out the sun here. It is unclear what the purpose of this trail may have been, but the disruption of the nature around you appears quite recent. Northwest leads to a sandy path, while southeast follows more broken and cut shrubbery.

36295.name: Shadowy Trail
36295.description: Pushing through the shrubs and ferns, you find it difficult to determine where you've been or where you're going as it all looks the same. The only indicitive sign is that of loosely distrubed fern limbs and spaciously cut branches of shrubbery. Maybe coming this way wasn't such a good idea. The trail appears to continue east and northwest here, you aren't sure.

36296.name: Sandy Beach Trail
36296.description: The trail is densely covered in ferns and bushes, making it difficult to determine your footing. Wildlife flees into hiding as you disturb the area, as it proves difficult to move about in any sort of subtle manner. The trail continues west and opens up to the beach to the northeast.

36299.name: On the Beach
36299.description: You stand before a large tent to the west as Palekanian forces pour in from the shores to the east. The ocean extends out to the east, while a large rock formation blocks any passage to the south. Dense trees and ferns behind the tent give it coverage from the city, allowing a safe spot for rallying forces to regain their bearings. The beach continues to the north.

36300.name: Off the Coast
36300.description: Passing by the swells, you paddle closer to the large vessel. A rope ladder offers access to the deck as it floats, anchored against the current. Not a very large craft, but sufficient enough to transport a sizable army. Paddling southwest leads to the beach.

36301.name: On the Beach
36301.description: A large ship can be seen immediately to the northeast, as large dinghies are shored here after ferrying soldiers to the island. Soldiers split off in both directions along the coast as orders are given out by commanding officers. South appears less chaotic as the Palekanian soldiers rally before a tent.

36303.name: On the Beach
36303.description: The beach bends to the west and southeast here. Dinghies are shored along the coast in front of a large vessel further out in the ocean. The sands beneath your feet are extremely disturbed by the heavy foot traffic.

36306.name: On the Beach
36306.description: The beach opens up to a clearing, breaking away from the noise of clanging steel and warcries. A gentle breeze comes in from the north, offering a sweet scent of salt and brine. North leads out to the vast ocean, while east brings you to the front of the battle.

36307.name: On the Beach
36307.description: The beach is overwhelmed by soldiers engaged in combat here. Palekanian soldiers charging in from the east are met by the mismatched hired guard of Kulani. It would be wise to not be in the middle of it. The clanging of steel on steel proves overbearing as arrows rain down on the area. The beach continues east and west here, offering much of the same. To the north lies a vast ocean.

36309.name: On the Beach
36309.description: Absolute chaos. The battle before you is disorganized at best. Looking for some sort of leading figure, you only see mismatched mercenaries defending against the soldiers of Palekana. Bloodied corpses lie among other bodies calling for aid. Arrows stick out of the sand as the beach continues to the east and west here. South leads towards a narrow sandy trail to the city, while north lies a vast ocean.

36311.name: Path to the Northern Shore
36311.description: The sandy path is congested with heavy foot traffic as armed men and women trample the ground here. The sands display a red hue from blood that has been kicked around. The mahogany and koa trees end to the north where the beach can be seen. South leads towards the northern arch of Kulani, and ferns congest the base of the trees on either side of you.

36313.name: The Council Chamber
36313.description: Large marble pillars encompass the chamber in a circle around you. Stairs which double as stage seating escalate in all directions except for the exits. The entire room is enclosed in stone walls engraved with decorative patterns, giving texture to the otherwise bare stone. Looking up, the ceiling extends into a large dome with stained glass windows circling the curvature above you. The vanity of this chamber is clear. The arched entrances to northeast and southeast lead out of the chamber. A door can be seen above the steps to the west.

36314.name: Southern Vestibule
36314.description: The vestibule is a simple, large antechamber with stone columns leading up to the main venue. The red carpeting ends here, as the stone flooring continues northwest between escalating stair seating on either side of the entrance. East offers a carpeted gallery of paintings lit by candle sconces. A stained glass window embeded in the south wall gives light to the area.

36315.name: Northern Hallway
36315.description: The vestibule is a simple, large antechamber with stone columns leading up to the main venue. The red carpeting ends here, as the stone flooring continues southwest between escalating stair seating on either side of the entrance. East offers a carpeted gallery of paintings lit by candle sconces. A stained glass window embeded in the north wall gives light to the area.

36318.name: The Kitchen
36318.description: The north wall of the kitchen is lined with various kitchen amenities. Bread proofers, herb boxes, knife and spice racks... everything one might need to begin preparations for a large meal. A massive table stands in the middle of the room covered in flour and herbs. A windowed metal door to the south glows with radiant warmth, smelling heavenly of freshly baked dough. The kitchen continues to the west.

36319.name: The Kitchen
36319.description: A sweet smell cooked meat and seared vegetables fills your nostrils as you enter. Stoves line the western wall separated by a deep sink in the middle made of marble. A massive table stands in the middle of the room for staging the food and other various preparations. A large windowed metal door to the south is cold to the touch, storing perishable goods, while east leads to more kitchen space. The kitchen exits to the north.

36320.name: The Gallery
36320.description: This southern hallway displays many landscape paintings, some of which look rather familiar to you. One depicting a dense forests behind a massive cityscape accompanied by a large body of water in the foreground appears quite like Mareldja. The gallery of paintings covering the walls bend to the north and west here. A sweet aroma a well-cooked food escapes from the seams of the door to the south.

36321.name: The Gallery
36321.description: The red carpeting leads to the west and south here, accompanied by a large number of scenic paintings. A rather wide painting catches your eye among the others. Vast mountain ranges lie behind a thick forest and a large city in the foreground. Atop the mountain range stands a rather large forest among the frozen tundra. The base of the mountains come to life with scattered illuminations of yellow and orange hue. A large mahogany door stands against the northern wall.

36322.name: Library
36322.description: Polished mahogany shelves cover the walls all around the room. The center of the room consists of shorter shelves standing waist-high and placed into aisles. Cushioned leather seating is offered along the northern wall on either side of the entrance. A wealth of information can certainly be found here.

36323.name: Director's Office
36323.description: A massive table stands in the center of the room here. Against the northern wall is a large desk consisting of several drawers with gold plated handles. Dozens of scrolls and books sitting atop the table are opened up displaying information about Kulani legislation and trade protocol. The walls are decorated with portrait paintings of high-class individuals, presumably of various nobles of Kulani. South leads out of the office to the main chamber.

36324.name: Council Meeting Room
36324.description: This large room hosts an enormous mahogany table with chairs pushed in all around it. A large map of the island can be seen against the far wall, displaying all of the native terrain and constructs. A few books and scrolls rest on top of the table, as well as some leftover bowls and plates from a recent meal. Paintings of landscapes and portraits line the walls between lit candle sconces, giving the room a pleasant setting. The room exits to the east.

36325.name: The Gallery
36325.description: Various paintings line the west wall on either side of a pair of large, mahogany doors and continuing to the north and south. The paintings consist of a wide range of landscapes; mountain foothills, river valleys, snowy tundras and beach sunsets depict an array of artistic visions. Red carpeting stretches in all directions here.

36326.name: Inside the Chamber
36326.description: Within the chamber, the marble flooring foundation lies beneath a fancy red carpet that leads in all directions. Along the northern and southern walls, sconces holding tall candles burn incessantly. Impressive scenic paintings hang between the candle lighting, continuing to the west. The carpet branches to the north and south, leading to polished mahogany doors.

36327.name: Before the Council Chamber
36327.description: Easily three stories tall, the chamber stands as a significant landmark for the island. Several grooved stone pillars support an arched awning leading to the entrance of the chamber. West leads into the chamber passed several rows of pillars. Looking east, you can see most of the city leading up to the eastern arched entrance to Kulani.

36328.name: Courtyard
36328.description: The massive circular stone structure before you stands tall atop a flight of stairs. The top of the building is domed with stained glass windows surrounding it. A grass lawn covers the area on either side of the cobblestone path with scattered dandelions swaying in the breeze. The courtyard carries a pleasant scent of flora as you bask in the scenery. The stairs lead west to the foot of the building, and east brings you under the arch.

36329.name: Before the Archway
36329.description: You stand before a massive arch that leads towards the largest structure in the city. A large garden accompanies the path to the west with various tropical flowers on either side. West leads passed the arch to the large stone structure, and east brings you to the western streets of Kulani. Along the wall, south leads to another structure with iron bars over the windows.

36330.name: Before the Arena
36330.description: Stunning! A large, oval stadium comprised of sand is encompassed by stage seating all around it. A wooden booth stands on the opposite end of the stadium overlooking the arena. A sign is displayed on a small wooden kiosk by the entrance to the stadium. North descends into the arena, while south leads to the western streets of the city.

36331.name: Before an Enormous Mahogany Tree
36331.description: This aged mahogany tree was preserved probably because of how massive it is. The tree offers shade that extends in all directions for yards. As materialistic as the island's inhabitants appear to be, it's hard to believe they kept this beauty around. All directions lead away from the tree.

36332.name: By the Pond
36332.description: A small, artificial pond is encased with stones of various shapes and sizes here. The crystalline water is so clear that you can see tropical fish swimming within it. The cobblestone path circles around a mahogany tree to the north.

36333.name: Park Path
36333.description: This corner of the park has a koa wood gazebo painted white sitting just off the trail. One can only imagine what kind of special events are hosted here. The cobblestone path circles to the south and west, around an old mahogany tree standing tall in the park center.

36334.name: Laccy's Delicacies
36334.description: This business operates more like a restaurant, as many of its customers choose to sit down, relax, and enjoy the tasty treats Laccy has for sale here. Koa wood tables and chairs are placed along the perimeter of the establishment, with only a few unoccupied seats available. Everyone indulging seems to be enjoying themselves. Why not join them? East leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

36335.name: Edrig's General Goods
36335.description: While the recent events may have dwindled some of his stock, the owner does his best to compensate and recover the shelves. Everyday items ranging from liquid containers, rations, candles and other perishables can be found for a bargain here. West leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

36336.name: Blue Silken Tent
36336.description: This tent appears to be strictly dedicated to clothing and fashionable accessories. Suits, robes, dresses, hats and footwear are all found here in a very organized and segregated manner. Whether you need a sunhat for the beach, or hiking boots for rough exploration, this is the place to be. North leads to the steets of the bazaar.

36337.name: Golden Laced White Tent
36337.description: Inside the tent you find mahogany tables aligned into aisles with a path down the middle. On top of the tables are a plethora of gem encrusted items. Goblets, knives, sandals... if it exists, this vendor has found a way to put an expensive price tag on it. It seems to be working, as the tent is overcrowded with nobles and other flamboyant tourists. Finding something actually useful might be too few and far between. South leads to the streets of the bazaar.

36338.name: Black Silken Tent
36338.description: The absence of light within this tent is noticeable. Contrary to the other tents of the bazaar, this one carries a sense of gloom and despair. A pungent odor coming from within the tent wafts by you to the outside. Searching for the cause, you find potions, vials and beakers meticulously-aligned on the display tables within the tent. It smells awful. North leads to the streets of the bazaar.

36339.name: Plain White Tent
36339.description: Stepping into the tent, you are presented with countless gems, rocks and unbroken geodes. Perhaps you should try your luck and buy one. All minerals, even those nonindigenous to the island, are found here. You never know what you might get! The lighting in the tent is oriented in such a way that it gives sparkle to everything being displayed; it's almost hypnotizing. South leads to the streets of the bazaar.

36340.name: Makeshift Field Hospital
36340.description: This once-hostel has been transformed into an infirmary to accommodate the injured that have retreated from the battle ensuing to the north. Nearly every cot is occupied with soldiers suffering from various wounds, some with missing limbs and others with arrows still sticking out of their person. How awful. Robed men and women hurry about, assisting those in need of aid in a vaguely prioritized manner. The building exits to the west leading to the bloodied cobblestone streets.

36341.name: The Kulani Smithery
36341.description: The clanging of steel from multiple anvils being hammered upon barrages your ears as you enter. Forges burn brightly from being constantly billowed, and the sharpening wheels are in constant motion as the master smith and his apprentices attempt to keep up with the demand. This building probably wasn't designed for this sort of heavy operation. The heat of the forges mixed with the odor of sweat makes your stay here quite unpleasant. The doorway to the east leads out to the commotion on the cobblestone streets.

36342.name: Park Path
36342.description: The cobblestone path narrows and is accompanied by tall, koa wood planters with bright floral arrangements within them. Young koa and mahogany trees are planted periodically along the path on either side of you. The southern walls of Kulani are met with koa wood lattice fencing which encase the park. The fence itself is covered in beautifully green ivy that weaves itself around the entire perimeter. The path continues to the east and splits off to the north. A large, native mahogany tree sits in the center of the park.

36343.name: Ryley's Supply Shop
36343.description: This building is packed with aisles of mahogany display tables showcasing everything one might need for a quick visit to the beach, or an extended stay on the island. The walls are completely covered by shelves with an assortment of hand-made trinkets, beach ornaments and other knickknacks that might appeal to someone. Beach gear is exhibited by the windows at the front of the shop to entice passing tourists to make a day trip to the beach. The shop exits to the east.

36344.name: Park Path
36344.description: Young koa and mahogany trees accompany potted ferns and floral arrangements as the cobblestone path circles around the park. The lattice fence corners here extending to the east and south. A massive mahogany tree stands at the park center to the southeast.

36345.name: Liani's Flowers and Fruits
36345.description: The sweet auroma of flowers lingers in the air here as rows of potted flowers sit on the tables before you. Tiered display crates of fruits line the western wall consisting of various sweet fruits; apples, grapes and mangos being among them. You can see that everything is well stocked and immediately recovered to keep the display tables as attractive as possible. The shop exits to the east.

36346.name: The Pub
36346.description: A long, polished mahogany bar extends from north to south at the far side of the room with an insane amount of liquor variety shelved behind it. Tables and barstools are nearly filled as the tourists and locals congregate together sharing stories of their adventures or the latest gossip. You can spot one available table and a couple barstools next to some cackling patrons. The room exits to the east, leading back the the inn's lobby.

36347.name: Alon's Outfitters
36347.description: Standing before the counter, you notice a small anvil and forge in one corner while a sharpening wheel sits alongside an elongated mahogany table with various sewing and stitching supplies. A sign hangs on the east wall. North leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

36348.name: Royal Bank of Kulani
36348.description: The interior design does not match the outer structure's design at all. The flooring is entirely made up of marble. Koa wood counters stretch from east to west, wall to wall, with ropes attached to platinum poles extending from each window. Best pick a line now if you want service for your banking needs. South leads out of the building to the cobblestone streets.

36352.name: Suite 202
36352.description: This room is very large. It stretches from north to south with windows that give the tenant a view of the streets and the beach resepectively. Two large beds sit against the eastern wall with dressers and clothes cabinets along the western wall. This room appears to be occupied by several adults, as suggested by the two beds, and by how completely filled the cabinets and dressers are. The room exits to the west.

36361.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
36361.description: The carpeted western hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the east, where the second floor's balcony overlooks the lobby.

36362.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
36362.description: The carpeted eastern hallway ends with three doors leading into rooms of the inn. Mahogany doors surround you in all directions except to the west, where the second floor's balcony overlooks the lobby.

36363.name: Kulani Suites - 2nd Floor
36363.description: The marble flooring is lined with a red carpet that stretches east to west while a railing to the north overlooks the inn's lobby below. The staircase leads down to the first floor of the inn. East and west offer rooms to the second floor of the inn.

36392.name: Fishing Hut
36392.description: Made entirely out of koa wood, this hut is decorated with rods, tackle and bait all strewn about haphazardly. You feel the sudden urge to pick up a fishing hobby just gazing at the sheer amount of fishing goods found here. A counter in the corner of the room encases the more exotic baits and tackle.

36413.name: Lighthouse - 3rd Floor
36413.description: The surrounding walls are made up of glass and open windows. The breeze from the outside sweeps in strongly to the opposite window and down the opening to the floor below. The platform is made up of sturde koa wood and at the center stands a large, bright beacon that rotates clockwise. Faint, ominous whispers can be heard from all around you.

36419.name: Battle Arena
36419.description: An oval-shaped dirt arena is surrounded by stone bleachers that overlook the area. A noisy crowd of onlookers cheer wildly as an announcer's voice booms across the stadium.

1114.name: Aboard the Caravel
1114.description: The vessel is constructed from high grade oak and pine. Two large triangular sails are hoisted into the air at both the stern and amidship. A flag waves at the top of the central sail representing Mareldja's crown. You are accompanied by numerous other citizens and sailors that operate the craft.

36451.name: Within the Enormous Mahogany Tree
36451.description: The tree ascends for tens of feet above the nearest building as it towers over the city. The branches sway from the salty breeze sweeping in from the south. Clambering back down will require significant skill.

npcs.name: Komura
npcs.description: The shifty elf is perched on one of the branches overseeing the city. He appears quite focused on whatever it is he is doing, keeping his attention focused towards the east.

36271.name: Inside the Cave
36271.description: The cave opens up to a massive cavern. Darkness shrouds every direction of travel except for the way out. Sound carries off to a seemingly endless distance. It is difficult to determine if walking in any direction would be a good idea.

36284.name: The Stern
36284.description: The stern of the vessel faces out towards the ocean, giving you a beautiful view of the vast ocean. The ship descends down towards the port and starboard side, while a staircase can be seen leading down into the hull of the ship.

36285.name: Starboard Side of the Stern
36285.description: The starboard side is railed off, looking west towards the beach of the island. Peering out, you can see soldiers charging into battle with swords drawn on the shore.

36286.name: Forward Starboard Side
36286.description: The starboard side is railed off, looking west towards the beach of the island. Peering out, you can see soldiers charging into battle with swords drawn on the shore.

36287.name: The Bow
36287.description: The bow of the ship faces the island, to which only the northern shore is visible with the island's treeline obstructing anything beyond it. The boat sways gently here as the anchored vessel submits to the current.

36288.name: Starboard Side of the Cog
36288.description: On the main deck of the ship stands a single mast with raised sails. Over the starboard of the ship, you can see the northern beaches of Kulani. Several ropes lie coiled here from where the vessel had launched its dinghies.

36289.name: Port Side of the Stern
36289.description: This side of the vessel offers a stunning view of the sparkling ocean that continues as far as the eye can see. Looking south, you can see a large rock formation that protrudes out from the island.

36290.name: Forward Port Side
36290.description: This side of the vessel offers a stunning view of the sparkling ocean that continues as far as the eye can see. Looking south, you can see a large rock formation that protrudes out from the island.

36291.name: Port Side of the Cog
36291.description: On the main deck of the ship stands a single mast with raised sails. Over the port side of the ship, the vast ocean stretches as far as the eye can see. A small rope ladder extends over the side of the vessel, offering a way down to the ocean.

36310.name: Among the Trees
36310.description: Large branches extend up and outwards in all directions here, offering plenty of ways to gain footing. Down leads to a sandy path with heavy foot traffic.

36349.name: Suite 205
36349.description: This room is very large. It stretches from north to south with windows that give the tenant a view of the streets and the beach resepectively. One large bed sits against the western wall with dressers and clothes cabinet along the eastern wall. This room appears vacant with no sign of personal belongings. The room exits to the east.

36350.name: Suite 204
36350.description: This second floor room offers an absolutely breathtaking view of the beach. The room's bed sits against the northern wall and is aligned with the window overlooking the beach. Dressers sit on either side of the window. Atop one of the dressers lies a wooden crucifix next to a brown book with gold writing etched into the cover. The room exits to the north.

36351.name: Suite 206
36351.description: This room offers a view that overlooks the streets of Kulani, and a peek at the northern side of the island. Covered by a layer of trees, only the open sea can be seen. A bed sits against the eastern wall of the room, and a couple dressers occupy the western wall. The belongings within the room suggest it is occupied by a noblewoman, as heels and gaudy sandals are aligned by the door, and colorful sundresses and skirts are hung within the clothing cabinet. The room exits to the south.

npcs.name: Winry
npcs.description: The hooded elf in green robes paces around the room in quiet haste. She affixes several holy trinkets around the room to bolster good omens and energy. As quickly as she moves around, she is unnaturally quiet.
